title: capsella bursa-pastoris subsp. gracilis
intro: Capsella bursa-pastoris subsp. gracilis, also known as shepherd's purse, is a plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its small, white, four-petaled flowers and heart-shaped seed pods. Traditionally, shepherd's purse has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic properties. The medicinal properties of shepherd's purse include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for colds, sore throats, and skin conditions. Shepherd's purse can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
